metadata.name: ACTES CAR-T Component Library
metadata.source: InSynBio ACTES — UniProt REST, published literature, patents

tier_definitions.T1: FDA/EMA-approved in CAR-T product; sequence clinically validated
tier_definitions.T2: Published in peer-reviewed clinical trial; IND-filed or Phase I/II
tier_definitions.T3: Research-stage; published in peer-reviewed literature only

sequence_status_codes.STUB: No sequence yet; source documented for fetch
sequence_status_codes.LENGTH_MISMATCH: Sequence present but length differs from literature value

elements.name: CD8α Signal Peptide
elements.tier_justification: Used in Kymriah, Yescarta, Abecma, Carvykti, Breyanzi, Tecartus

usage_context.role: Drives efficient surface expression of CAR construct

qa.source: P01732 (CD8A_HUMAN) res 1-21

elements.design_notes: Default leader for CAR-T vectors. Proven efficiency across all major approved products.

elements.name: GM-CSF Signal Peptide
elements.tier_justification: Used in multiple published CAR-T constructs; high secretion efficiency

usage_context.role: Alternative leader with higher ER insertion efficiency for some constructs

qa.source: P04141 (CSF2_HUMAN) res 1-17

elements.design_notes: GM-CSF (CSF2) signal peptide (aa 1-22) drives efficient protein secretion in mammalian cells. Widely used for secreted CAR payloads (cytokines, bispecifics) and scFv surface display. Superior expression in T cells vs IgG kappa SP for secreted proteins.

elements.name: Granulin Signal Peptide
elements.tier_justification: Research-stage alternative; strong ER targeting

usage_context.role: Alternative signal peptide for experimental constructs

qa.source: P28799 (GRN_HUMAN) res 1-21

elements.design_notes: Granulin/Epithelin signal peptide (GRN, aa 1-21) provides efficient secretion in T cells. Used in armored CAR constructs for cytokine payload secretion. UniProt P28799; alternative to GM-CSF SP with comparable efficiency.

gene_annotation.element_description: Granulin signal peptide (aa 1-21)

elements.name: IgG Kappa Light Chain Signal Peptide
elements.tier_justification: Used in many published bispecific and scFv constructs

usage_context.role: Standard antibody secretion leader; pairs naturally with scFv binders

qa.source: [Boundary: Residues 1-22 (22aa)]  [Gene: IGKC, 3514]  P01834 (IGKC_HUMAN) canonical signal peptide

elements.design_notes: Immunoglobulin kappa light chain signal peptide (aa 1-22) drives efficient surface display of scFv-based CAR constructs. Standard SP for most clinical CAR-T binder domains. Used in tisagenlecleucel (Kymriah), axicabtagene ciloleucel (Yescarta), and most approved CARs.

gene_annotation.boundary_note: IgG kappa constant region N-terminal SP: res 1-22 (22aa signal peptide)

elements.name: IL-2 Signal Peptide
elements.tier_justification: Research-stage; used in some secreted payload constructs

usage_context.role: Secretion leader for IL-2 or other cytokine payloads in armored CAR

qa.source: P60568 (IL2_HUMAN) res 1-20

elements.design_notes: CAR-T usage: indications; cell_types; role. See qa.source for sequence provenance.

elements.name: CD8α Short Hinge
elements.tier_justification: Used in Kymriah, Abecma, Carvykti, Breyanzi (4-1BB-based products)

usage_context.role: Membrane-proximal epitope targeting (e.g., GD2, CD19, BCMA, GPC3)

qa.source: P01732 (CD8A_HUMAN) res 138-182

elements.design_notes: Rule: epitope-membrane distance <5nm → CD8α Short. Avoids steric clash. Key for solid tumor antigens with membrane-proximal epitopes.

elements.name: CD8α Long Hinge
elements.tier_justification: Published in multiple CAR-T papers for membrane-distal epitopes

usage_context.role: Membrane-distal or glycocalyx-shielded epitopes

qa.source: P01732 (CD8A_HUMAN) res 90-210

elements.design_notes: Use when epitope is buried under glycocalyx or membrane-distal (>10nm). Risk: excess flexibility may reduce synapse quality.

elements.name: CD28 Medium Hinge
elements.tier_justification: Used in Yescarta and Tecartus (CD28-based products)

usage_context.role: Standard lipid-raft-targeting hinge for CD28 scaffolds

elements.design_notes: Use with CD28 TM domain for synergistic lipid raft localization. Consistent 39aa length across all CD28-based approved products.

elements.name: IgG4 Long Hinge (S228P, Fc-null)
elements.tier_justification: Multiple published CAR-T papers; S228P prevents half-antibody formation

usage_context.role: Long-reach hinge for membrane-distal antigens with stability improvement

qa.source: P01861 (IGHG4_HUMAN) EU 216-447; S228P mutation

elements.design_notes: S228P (Labrijn et al. Nat Biotechnol 2009) prevents disulfide shuffling. Add LALA (L234A/L235A) to eliminate residual Fc binding. Use for HER2, EGFR, and other membrane-distal solid tumor targets.
elements.mutation_note: S228P applied at EU228 (P01861 position 108). LALA should be added if Fc-null required.

elements.name: IgD Hinge (Protease-Resistant)
elements.tier_justification: Emerging; protease-resistant hinge for solid tumor/TME with high protease activity

usage_context.role: Alternative long hinge with TME protease resistance

qa.source: P01880 (IGHD_HUMAN) res ~100-163

elements.design_notes: MMP/ADAM-resistant compared to IgG4. Consider for pancreatic/ovarian CAR-T where TME proteases degrade standard hinges.

elements.name: CD8α Transmembrane Domain
elements.tier_justification: Used in Kymriah, Abecma, Carvykti, Breyanzi

usage_context.role: Low tonic signaling; stable membrane anchoring

qa.source: P01732 (CD8A_HUMAN) res 183-206

elements.design_notes: Preferred when reducing tonic signaling to prevent exhaustion in solid tumors. Pairs with CD8α short hinge and 4-1BB costimulation.
references: Brentjens et al. Nat Med 2003; PMID:14765119

elements.name: CD28 Transmembrane Domain
elements.tier_justification: Used in Yescarta, Tecartus
clinical_trials: Used in Yescarta (axicabtagene) and many others

usage_context.role: Lipid-raft localization; higher tonic signaling baseline

elements.design_notes: Lipid-raft recruitment enhances CD28-CD3ζ complex formation. Higher tonic signal → risk of exhaustion in chronic antigen exposure (solid tumors).
references: Maher et al. Nat Biotechnol 2002; PMID:11875433

elements.name: CD4 Transmembrane Domain
elements.tier_justification: Used in published CAR-NK and some CAR-T constructs

usage_context.role: Alternative TM for NK-optimized constructs

qa.source: P01730 (CD4_HUMAN) res 397-418

elements.design_notes: Used in early CAR constructs (1st gen). CD4 TM has lower lipid-raft affinity; may reduce tonic signal in some NK contexts.

gene_annotation.element_description: CD4 transmembrane domain (aa 397-418)

elements.name: CD3ζ Transmembrane Domain
elements.tier_justification: Used in SynNotch hybrids and non-signaling anchor applications

usage_context.role: TM for CD3ζ full-chain constructs; SynNotch hybrids

qa.source: P20963 (CD247_HUMAN) res 22-51

elements.design_notes: Used when building full CD3ζ chain including TM. In standard CAR-T, CD3ζ TM is replaced by CD8α or CD28 TM with CD3ζ cytoplasmic tail only.

gene_annotation.element_description: CD3ζ transmembrane domain (aa 31-51)

elements.name: NKG2D Transmembrane Domain
elements.tier_justification: CAR-NK NKG2D-based constructs

usage_context.role: Natural NK receptor TM for NKG2D-based CAR-NK designs

qa.source: P26718 (NKG2D_HUMAN) res ~175-216

elements.design_notes: Pairs with NKG2D ectodomain for stress-ligand sensing CAR-NK. Associates with DAP10/DAP12 adapters for NK-specific signaling.

elements.name: 4-1BB (CD137) Costimulatory Domain
elements.tier_justification: Kymriah, Abecma, Carvykti, Breyanzi — 42aa cytoplasmic domain

usage_context.role: Memory formation, mitochondrial metabolism, T-cell persistence

qa.source: Q07011 (TNFR9_HUMAN) res 214-255

elements.design_notes: Preferred over CD28 for durable responses and reduced CRS peak. TRAF1/2 signaling → mitochondrial biogenesis → memory T cells. Rule: 4-1BB for solid tumors (persistence) and hematologic relapse prevention.
references: Imai et al. Leukemia 2004; PMID:14737178
Milone et al. Mol Ther 2009; PMID:19259074

elements.name: CD28 Costimulatory Domain
elements.tier_justification: Yescarta, Tecartus — glycolytic burst, rapid cytotoxicity

usage_context.role: Rapid T-cell activation, glycolytic burst, effector function

elements.design_notes: PI3K-AKT signaling → rapid effector differentiation. Higher CRS peak vs 4-1BB. Use for acute leukemias requiring fast response. Rule: CD28 for rapid-response hematologic malignancies (B-ALL, aggressive LBCL).
references: Maher et al. Nat Biotechnol 2002; PMID:11875433
Brentjens et al. Clin Cancer Res 2007; PMID:17363559

elements.name: OX40 (CD134) Costimulatory Domain
elements.tier_justification: Multiple clinical trials; enhanced persistence in solid tumors

usage_context.role: Enhanced T-cell survival, anti-apoptotic signaling, Th2/Tfh skewing

qa.source: P43489 (TNR4_HUMAN) res 238-277

elements.design_notes: TRAF2/3/5 signaling. Anti-apoptotic via NF-κB and PI3K. Use in 3rd-gen constructs (OX40+4-1BB or OX40+ICOS) for solid tumors. Key for CAR-Treg designs targeting autoimmune diseases.
references: Vera et al. J Immunother 2006; PMID:17033567

elements.name: ICOS Costimulatory Domain
elements.tier_justification: Clinical trials for follicular lymphoma and Treg CAR applications

usage_context.role: Th17/Th1 skewing, IL-10 production, Treg functional support

qa.source: Q9Y6W8 (ICOS_HUMAN) res 163-199

elements.design_notes: PI3Kδ-biased signaling → Th17/Th1 differentiation. Critical for CAR-Treg designs (ICOS promotes Treg suppressive function). In 3rd-gen: use OX40+ICOS for balanced persistence + effector function.
references: Guedan et al. J Clin Invest 2014; PMID:25250572

gene_annotation.element_description: ICOS cytoplasmic domain (aa 162-199); PI3K p85 binding (YMFM motif)

elements.name: 2B4 (CD244) Costimulatory Domain
elements.tier_justification: CAR-NK designs; enhances NK-cell activation without CAR-associated exhaustion

usage_context.role: SAP-dependent NK costimulation, ADCC enhancement

qa.source: Q9BZW8 (CD244_HUMAN) res 246-380
qa.validation_notes: Contains 4 ITSM (TxYxxV/I) motifs: confirmed by Q9BZW8 res 246-380; SAP/EAT-2 docking

elements.design_notes: SAP (SH2D1A) scaffolding protein required for activating signal. NK-specific: promotes degranulation and cytokine release without exhaustion. Rule: Use 2B4 in CAR-NK constructs over 4-1BB for NK biology compatibility.

elements.name: DAP12 (TYROBP) Costimulatory Adapter
elements.tier_justification: CAR-NK and CAR-M designs; ITAM-based NK/myeloid costimulation

usage_context.role: ITAM signaling adapter for NK and myeloid cell activation

qa.source: O43914 (TYROBP_HUMAN) res 76-106

elements.design_notes: DAP12 contains 1 ITAM; associates with NKG2C/D and KIR receptors. For CAR-NK: pairs with NKG2D-based CAR for optimal NK activation. For CAR-M: drives phagocytosis-coupled cytokine production.

elements.name: CD27 Costimulatory Domain
elements.tier_justification: Research-stage; provides unique Tfh and memory B-cell cooperation signals

usage_context.role: TRAF2/5 signaling, CD4+ T helper cooperation, anti-apoptotic

elements.design_notes: Less studied than 4-1BB/CD28; TRAF2/5 → NF-κB. May enhance cooperation between CAR-T and endogenous CD4+ T helpers.
references: Song et al. Blood 2012; PMID:22955919

gene_annotation.element_description: CD27 cytoplasmic domain (aa 236-260); TRAF2/5 binding

elements.name: CD3ζ Activation Domain (3×ITAM)
elements.tier_justification: All 6 FDA-approved CAR-T products (Kymriah/Yescarta/Abecma/Carvykti/Breyanzi/Tecartus)

usage_context.role: Primary killing signal via ZAP-70 recruitment; 3×ITAM = 6 YxxL/I motifs

elements.design_notes: Non-negotiable for standard CAR-T. 6 ITAM tyrosines (YxxL/I motifs) activate ZAP-70. Do NOT use full-length CD3ζ (include TM); only cytoplasmic tail (res 52-164). Consider 1XX variant (1 ITAM) for reduced exhaustion in chronic solid tumor setting.

elements.name: FcRγ Cytoplasmic Domain (CAR-M)
elements.tier_justification: CAR-Macrophage (CAR-M) for phagocytosis of solid tumors

usage_context.role: Phagocytic activation via Syk kinase; drives tumor cell engulfment

qa.source: P30273 (FCRG_HUMAN) res 45-86

elements.design_notes: Syk-dependent ITAM signaling in macrophages. Use with CD8α hinge/TM + CD3ζ excluded. CAR-M with FcRγ: Morrissey et al. Nat Biotechnol 2022. Induces M1 macrophage polarization + ECM remodeling for solid tumors.

elements.name: Truncated EGFR Safety Switch (Cetuximab-Targetable)
elements.tier_justification: Multiple Phase I/II trials (St. Jude, MSKCC); cetuximab-mediated ADCC elimination

usage_context.role: Cetuximab binds EGFR Domain III → NK-cell ADCC/CDC-mediated CAR-T elimination on demand

qa.source: P00533 (EGFR_HUMAN) SP(1-24)+DomIII(334-504)+DomIV(505-645)+TM(646-668); FIXED boundaries
qa.validation_notes: Domain III confirmed: KEITGF, AWPENR, VAFRGD motifs; NO kinase (LGGRR absent); TM: ALGIGLFM

elements.design_notes: CRITICAL: Assemble from SP+DIII+DIV+TM only. Kinase domain inclusion causes oncogenic transformation. Length should be ~313-359aa depending on exact boundaries. Cetuximab dose for elimination: 250mg/m². References: Wang X Blood 2011.
references: Wang et al. Blood 2011; PMID:21715312

elements.name: Inducible Caspase-9 (ΔCARD)
elements.tier_justification: NCT01494286 (Di Stasi NEJM 2011); >95% CAR-T clearance in <30 min with AP1903

usage_context.role: AP1903 (rimiducid)-induced FKBP12-F36V dimerization → Caspase-9 activation → apoptosis

qa.source: US8772039B2 (iCasp9 safety switch, Bellicum). WO2011/146862.  P55211 (CASP9_HUMAN) res 135-416 (ΔCARD, without CARD domain)
qa.validation_notes: Catalytic Cys at position 153 confirmed (FIQACGGEQK active site); GFGDVG N-terminal (ΔCARD)

elements.design_notes: ALWAYS pair with FKBP12-F36V (use F36V variant, not wild-type). Assembly: FKBP12-F36V-[G4S4]-iCasp9(ΔCARD). AP1903 (rimiducid) dose: 0.4mg/kg IV. Effect within 30 min. Di Stasi A et al. NEJM 2011;365:1673-83.
references: Di Stasi et al. NEJM 2011; PMID:22013103

elements.name: FKBP12 Dimerization Domain (iCasp9 partner)
elements.tier_justification: Component of iCasp9 safety switch system; use F36V variant for AP1903 specificity

usage_context.role: AP1903-induced homodimerization of FKBP12 → activates fused iCasp9

qa.source: [Boundary: Residues 1-108 (108aa)]  [Gene: FKBP1A, 2280]  P62942 (FKBP1A_HUMAN) full protein 108aa

elements.design_notes:
F36V mutation required for AP1903 (rimiducid) selectivity over endogenous FKBP ligands. Wild-type FKBP12 responds to both FK506 and AP1903; F36V is ~1000× more selective.
Also used in Split CAR: [FKBP12–Costim–CD3ζ] pairs with [Binder–Hinge–TM–FRB] for rapamycin-inducible CAR assembly (Wu CY Science 2015).
elements.mutation_note: F36V: Phe→Val at position 36. Critical for therapeutic use.

gene_annotation.boundary_note: FKBP1A_HUMAN full 108aa; no signal peptide; cytoplasmic protein

elements.name: RQR8 Dual Safety/Tracking Tag
elements.tier_justification: Autolus; combines CD34 enrichment (QBEnd10) + CD20 depletion (rituximab)

usage_context.role: QBEnd10 antibody enables GMP enrichment; rituximab enables on-demand CAR-T depletion

qa.source: Philip B et al. Blood 2014;124:1277 — PROPRIETARY AUTOLUS SEQUENCE. Exact AA sequence available in patent WO2014189489A1 (UCL/Autolus) Supplementary.

elements.design_notes: 73aa synthetic fusion: CD34 epitope(s) + CD20 mimotope(s). Exact sequence is Autolus proprietary (Philip B Blood 2014;124:1277). Functional approximation: [CD34-QBEnd10 epitope (16aa)] + [GSGSGS] + [CD20-mimotope (9aa)] x2. Obtain exact sequence from: Philip B, Carter JL et al. Blood 2014 Supplementary Table S1.

gene_annotation.boundary_note: Synthetic fusion: CD34 epitope (res 1-50) + CD20 epitope (res 51-146); Designed by Philip 2014 Blood; no natural UniProt/Gene ID — synthetic construct

references: Philip et al. Blood 2014; PMID:24755406

elements.name: Herpes Simplex Virus Thymidine Kinase (Suicide Gene)
elements.tier_justification: Earliest clinical suicide gene in T-cell therapy; ganciclovir-activated

usage_context.role: Ganciclovir conversion to toxic triphosphate form → DNA chain termination → cell death

qa.source: [Boundary: Residues 1-376 (376aa)]  [Gene: UL23, synthetic]  P06479 (TK1_HSVK1) full 376aa; Bonini C Science 1997;276:1719; ganciclovir-inducible cell death; NCT00027235 (Bonini BM transplant)

elements.design_notes: Original Bonini 1997 safety switch for DLI (donor lymphocyte infusion). HSV-TK phosphorylates ganciclovir (GCV) → toxic nucleotide → cell death. Advantage: potent, well-validated >25 years. Disadvantage: immunogenic (human anti-HSV-TK response), slower than iCasp9. iCasp9 now preferred for CAR-T. HSV-TK still used in some allogeneic T cell therapies.

gene_annotation.boundary_note: UL23 (TK_HHV11) HSV-1 thymidine kinase full 376aa; NCBI Gene: 2703454 (HHV-1 strain 17); UniProt P03176

references: Bonini et al. Science 1997; PMID:9160743

elements.name: FMC63 Anti-CD19 scFv (VH-G4S3-VL)
elements.tier_justification: Kymriah (tisagenlecleucel) and Breyanzi (lisocabtagene); gold standard CD19 binder

usage_context.role: CD19 recognition for B-ALL, LBCL, follicular lymphoma CAR-T therapy

qa.source: US8293490B2 (FMC63 antibody, Leinco Technologies/NCI); US9394368B2.  USPTO 9,701,758 B2 (Tisagenlecleucel); Nicholson IC et al. Mol Immunol 1997;34:1157

elements.design_notes: VH-(G4S)3-VL orientation. Kd ~0.5nM for human CD19. Binds CD19 epitope encompassing aa 41-196 of extracellular domain. Alternative: SJ25C1 scFv (used in earlier CD19 CARs). Key CDR: VH-CDR3 STYYGGDWYFNV; VL-CDR3 QQHYTTPPT.
references: Nicholson et al. Mol Immunol 1997; PMID:9383242

gene_annotation.element_description: FMC63 anti-CD19 scFv; VH+G4S3+VL; used in Kymriah

elements.name: SJ25C1 Anti-CD19 scFv (VH-G4S3-VL)
elements.tier_justification: Used in Axicabtagene ciloleucel (Yescarta) preclinical development

usage_context.role: Alternative CD19 binder; different epitope from FMC63

qa.source: PDB 6B9Z SJ25C1 anti-CD19; Brentjens RJ Nat Med 2003;9:279

elements.design_notes: Used in early NCI/Memorial Sloan Kettering CD19 CAR constructs. Distinguishable from FMC63 by CDR sequences; may show different kinetics.
references: Imai et al. Leukemia 2004; PMID:14737178

gene_annotation.element_description: SJ25C1 anti-CD19 scFv; VH+G4S3+VL

elements.name: c11D5.3 Anti-BCMA scFv (bb2121/Abecma precursor)
elements.tier_justification: bb2121 (idecabtagene vicleucel, Abecma, FDA 2021) — first BCMA CAR-T approved

usage_context.role: BCMA recognition for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma

qa.source: PDB 7KH0 (bb2121/c11D5.3 anti-BCMA CAR complex); Shi H et al. Cell 2020;183:1043

elements.design_notes: Fully human scFv from phage display. Kd ~3.4nM. First approved BCMA-targeted CAR. Competes with GPRC5D (ciltacabtagene) in myeloma. VH-CDR3: GTGYYGMDV; VL: kappa chain.

elements.name: JNJ-68284528 Biepitopic BCMA VHH (Carvykti)
elements.tier_justification: Ciltacabtagene autoleucel (Carvykti, FDA 2022) — highest response rate BCMA CAR-T

usage_context.role: Biepitopic BCMA recognition (two tandem VHH domains) for ultra-high avidity

qa.source: Representative BCMA VHH (J22.9 class from published anti-BCMA nanobody literature); Hultberg JBC 2015 anti-BCMA nanobody. Carvykti uses biepitopic VHH1+VHH2 — exact sequences: patent CN109485732B.
qa.status: Representative BCMA VHH — not the exact Carvykti sequence

elements.design_notes: NOTE: This is a representative BCMA-targeting VHH (J22.9 class, ~122aa). Carvykti (cilta-cel) uses 2 proprietary BCMA VHHs (VHHBCMA1+VHHBCMA2) in tandem (~280aa). For reference CAR design: use this as a template BCMA VHH. For clinical-grade Carvykti construct: obtain from Legend Biotech license.

elements.name: Trastuzumab Anti-HER2 scFv (VH-G4S3-VL)
elements.tier_justification: HER2 CAR-T trials (caution: affinity attenuation required for normal tissue safety)

usage_context.role: HER2 recognition for gastric, breast, ovarian, osteosarcoma CAR-T

qa.source: PDB 1N8Z chains B(VH) + A(VL); Carter P et al. PNAS 1992;89:4285; US5821337

elements.design_notes: CRITICAL: Full-affinity Kd ~0.1nM causes fatal on-target/off-tumor lung toxicity (Morgan RA et al. Mol Ther 2010). MUST attenuate affinity to Kd ~50-500nM via CDR mutagenesis (e.g., T28A in VH) for safe solid tumor use. AlphaFold: HER2-ECD is membrane-distal → use IgG4 Long hinge.
elements.mutation_note: Affinity attenuation required. Target Kd: 50-500nM. T28A (VH CDR1) commonly used.
elements.fetch_note: PDB 1N8Z chain B (VH 120aa) + G4S3 + chain A (VL 107aa) = 242aa

elements.name: Daratumumab Anti-CD38 scFv
elements.tier_justification: CD38 CAR-T for multiple myeloma; daratumumab patent-derived scFv

usage_context.role: CD38 recognition for myeloma; also active on NK cells (toxicity consideration)

qa.source: US9732154B2 (daratumumab, Genmab/Janssen). WO2006099875A2.  PDB 4CMH (Daratumumab Fab); de Weers M et al. J Immunol 2011;186:1840

elements.design_notes: CD38 expressed on NK cells → daratumumab-derived CAR may cause NK fratricide. Combine with NK CD38 knockout for allogeneic settings. Use with iCasp9 safety switch in myeloma where CD38+ normal hematopoiesis at risk.

elements.name: SS1 Anti-Mesothelin scFv
elements.tier_justification: Multiple Phase I/II solid tumor CAR-T trials (mesothelioma, ovarian, NSCLC)

usage_context.role: Mesothelin recognition for mesothelioma, ovarian, pancreatic, lung CAR-T

qa.source: PDB 3O2D SS1(Fv) anti-mesothelin; Hassan R J Immunol 2002; Chang K Pastan I PNAS 1996;93:136; clinical: amatuximab basis

elements.design_notes: Murine-derived; humanization may improve persistence. Mesothelin overexpressed on solid tumors but expressed on normal mesothelium (pleura, peritoneum) → monitor for on-target off-tumor toxicity. Prefer regional delivery (intrapleural, intraperitoneal).

elements.name: 14G2a Humanized Anti-GD2 scFv
elements.tier_justification: GD2 CAR-T for neuroblastoma, SCLC, TNBC, osteosarcoma

usage_context.role: GD2 recognition for neuroblastoma and other GD2+ solid tumors

qa.source: hu14.18 (humanized 14G2a) VH/VL; Gillies SD mAbs 1993; Dinutuximab (Unituxin FDA 2015) uses same CDRs; CAR-T: Heczey A JCI 2017 (neuroblastoma)

elements.design_notes: hu14.18 (humanized 14G2a) — same CDRs as dinutuximab (FDA 2015 for neuroblastoma). GD2 highly expressed: neuroblastoma, osteosarcoma, melanoma, small cell lung cancer. CAR-T superior to mAb for solid tumor penetration (Heczey 2017 JCI). Pair with IL-15/IL-7 armor for solid tumor microenvironment resistance.

elements.name: m971 Humanized Anti-CD22 scFv (Membrane-Proximal Epitope)
elements.tier_justification: Anti-CD22 CAR-T: NCT02315612, NCT02443831

usage_context.role: CD22 recognition; membrane-proximal epitope preserves activity after CD22 splice variant escape

qa.source: PDB 3KJ4 anti-CD22 VH/VL; anti-CD22 RFB4 class antibody; Haso W Blood 2013;121:1165 (m971 reference)

elements.design_notes: Pairs with FMC63 (CD19) for bi-specific anti-escape strategy in B-ALL. CD22 membrane-proximal epitope — use CD8α Short or CD28 Medium hinge.
elements.role_in_car: CD22 binder for dual CD19+CD22 strategy

elements.name: OKT3 (Muromonab) Anti-CD3ε scFv — Murine/Reference
elements.tier_justification: Muromonab (OKT3) is FDA-approved anti-CD3 mAb (1986, transplant rejection). scFv format used in blinatumomab and CD3-bispecific CAR-T

usage_context.role: CD3ε-targeting for BiTE formats, T-cell engagers, or CD3-based logic gate components

qa.source: NCBI P01786 (VH 117aa) + P01829 (VL 121aa mature); muromonab-CD3 FDA 1986; Chatenoud L Nat Rev Immunol 2003;3:123

elements.design_notes: OKT3 VH/VL used in blinatumomab (anti-CD3 arm) and Treg-CAR-T constructs. Murine sequence — humanize before clinical use. Humanized OKT3γ1 (Ala-Ala): NCT00627835. For CAR-Treg: pairs with DSG3/MuSK binder for antigen-specific Treg activation.
elements.role_in_car: Anti-CD3ε binder for T cell engagement or Treg CAR

elements.name: YP7 Humanized Anti-GPC3 scFv
elements.tier_justification: GPC3 CAR-T for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC); multiple China Phase I/II trials

usage_context.role: GPC3 recognition for HCC CAR-T; TGFB_DNR armor recommended in HCC TME

qa.source: PDB 5XJ3 anti-GPC3; Feng M PNAS 2013;110:E4083 (YP7); GPC3-CAR-T Phase I: NCT02414126 (liver HCC)

elements.design_notes: YP7 (human scFv) binds GPC3 C-terminal domain. GPC3 highly expressed in HCC (70-90%), hepatoblastoma, ovarian yolk sac. Recommended for GPC3-CAR-T in liver cancer (NCT02414126, NCT03302403). Short hinge preferred (GPC3 membrane-proximal epitope).

elements.name: NKG2D Ectodomain (Stress-Ligand Binder)
elements.tier_justification: CAR-NK/T with NKG2D-based recognition of MICA/MICB/ULBP on stressed/cancer cells

usage_context.role: Natural stress-ligand recognition (MICA, MICB, ULBP1-6) — broad tumor targeting without scFv

qa.source: P26718 (NKG2D_HUMAN/KLRK1) res 73-216 ectodomain

elements.design_notes: Broad-spectrum tumor recognition via upregulated stress ligands. No single-target dependency → harder for tumor to escape via antigen loss. Use NKG2D ECD as binder + DAP10/DAP12 TM+cytoplasmic in CAR-NK design. Ferreira et al. Nat Med 2017;23:1379.

elements.name: APRIL Ligand Fragment (BCMA+TACI Dual Targeting)
elements.tier_justification: Emerging; APRIL naturally binds both BCMA and TACI → dual-antigen targeting

usage_context.role: BCMA and TACI dual recognition via natural ligand-receptor interaction

qa.source: O75888 (APRIL=TNFSF13_HUMAN) res 105-250 TNF-homology domain; Guo B JCI 2016;126:4295 (BCMA+TACI dual CART); Schmidts A Leukemia 2019; BINDS BOTH BCMA and TACI — prevents antigen escape in MM

MAGE-A4 is a cancer-testis antigen expressed in: melanoma (50%), NSCLC (20%), bladder cancer (35%), H&N SCC (30%), gastroesophageal cancer (20%). elements.design_notes: LOCKR: computationally designed de novo protein switch. Cage protein (~84aa) sequesters signaling domain. Key protein (~65aa) outcompetes → releases. CAR-T application: LOCKR-gated CD3ζ — signaling released only when Key (synthetic tag) present. Provides clean AND-gate logic: tumor antigen + Key presence → CAR activation. Boyken 2019 Science: demonstrated in cell-free and cell-based systems. For sequences: PDB 6NNQ (Cage), 6NNS (Key); de novo designed, no natural homolog.
